summary:
Circular RNAs are primarily formed by back splicing of exons, where the 3’ end of one exon fuses to the 5’ end of another upstream exon, creating a closed loop structure. Although the retrieved materials do not explicitly discuss back splicing, established biochemical and genomic evidence confirms this well-documented mechanism for exon-derived circRNA production.  

\boxed{\text{A}}
